verses.latin_vulgate: Exsultate Deo adjutori nostro ;  jubilate Deo Jacob.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֭רְנִינוּ לֵאלֹהִ֣ים עוּזֵּ֑נוּ הָ֝רִ֗יעוּ לֵאלֹהֵ֥י יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.king_james: Sing aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sumite psalmum, et date tympanum ;  psalterium jucundum cum cithara.
verses.king_james: Take a psalm, and bring hither the timbrel, the pleasant harp with the psaltery.

verses.latin_vulgate: Buccinate in neomenia tuba,  in insigni die solemnitatis vestræ :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּקְע֣וּ בַחֹ֣דֶשׁ שׁוֹפָ֑ר בַּ֝כֵּ֗סֶה לְי֣וֹם חַגֵּֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Blow up the trumpet in the new moon, in the time appointed, on our solemn feast day.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia præceptum in Israël est,  et judicium Deo Jacob.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י חֹ֣ק לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֣ל ה֑וּא מִ֝שְׁפָּ֗ט לֵאלֹהֵ֥י יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.king_james: For this was a statute for Israel, and a law of the God of Jacob.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testimonium in Joseph posuit illud,  cum exiret de terra Ægypti ;  linguam quam non noverat, audivit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵד֤וּת ׀ בִּיה֘וֹסֵ֤ף שָׂמ֗וֹ בְּ֭צֵאתוֹ עַל־אֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרָ֑יִם שְׂפַ֖ת לֹא־יָדַ֣עְתִּי אֶשְׁמָֽע׃
verses.king_james: This he ordained in Joseph for a testimony, when he went out through the land of Egypt: where I heard a language that I understood not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divertit ab oneribus dorsum ejus ;  manus ejus in cophino servierunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲסִיר֣וֹתִי מִסֵּ֣בֶל שִׁכְמ֑וֹ כַּ֝פָּ֗יו מִדּ֥וּד תַּעֲבֹֽרְנָה׃
verses.king_james: I removed his shoulder from the burden: his hands were delivered from the pots.

verses.latin_vulgate: In tribulatione invocasti me, et liberavi te.  Exaudivi te in abscondito tempestatis ;  probavi te apud aquam contradictionis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּצָּרָ֥ה קָרָ֗אתָ וָאֲחַ֫לְּצֶ֥ךָּ אֶ֭עֶנְךָ בְּסֵ֣תֶר רַ֑עַם אֶבְחׇנְךָ֨ עַל־מֵ֖י מְרִיבָ֣ה סֶֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: Thou calledst in trouble, and I delivered thee; I answered thee in the secret place of thunder: I proved thee at the waters of Meribah. Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audi, populus meus, et contestabor te.  Israël, si audieris me,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמַ֣ע עַ֭מִּי וְאָעִ֣ידָה בָּ֑ךְ יִ֝שְׂרָאֵ֗ל אִם־תִּֽשְׁמַֽע־לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Hear, O my people, and I will testify unto thee: O Israel, if thou wilt hearken unto me;

verses.latin_vulgate: non erit in te deus recens,  neque adorabis deum alienum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יִהְיֶ֣ה בְ֭ךָ אֵ֣ל זָ֑ר וְלֹ֥א תִ֝שְׁתַּחֲוֶ֗ה לְאֵ֣ל נֵכָֽר׃
verses.king_james: There shall no strange god be in thee; neither shalt thou worship any strange god.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego enim sum Dominus Deus tuus,  qui eduxi te de terra Ægypti.  Dilata os tuum, et implebo illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָֽנֹכִ֨י ׀ יְ֘הֹוָ֤ה אֱלֹהֶ֗יךָ הַֽ֭מַּעַלְךָ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרָ֑יִם הַרְחֶב־פִּ֝֗יךָ וַאֲמַלְאֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non audivit populus meus vocem meam,  et Israël non intendit mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־שָׁמַ֣ע עַמִּ֣י לְקוֹלִ֑י וְ֝יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל לֹא־אָ֥בָה לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: But my people would not hearken to my voice; and Israel would none of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dimisi eos secundum desideria cordis eorum ;  ibunt in adinventionibus suis.
verses.king_james: So I gave them up unto their own hearts’ lust: and they walked in their own counsels.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si populus meus audisset me,  Israël si in viis meis ambulasset,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ל֗וּ עַ֭מִּי שֹׁמֵ֣עַֽ לִ֑י יִ֝שְׂרָאֵ֗ל בִּדְרָכַ֥י יְהַלֵּֽכוּ׃
verses.king_james: Oh that my people had hearkened unto me, and Israel had walked in my ways!

verses.latin_vulgate: pro nihilo forsitan inimicos eorum humiliassem,  et super tribulantes eos misissem manum meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֭מְעַט אוֹיְבֵיהֶ֣ם אַכְנִ֑יעַ וְעַ֥ל צָ֝רֵיהֶ֗ם אָשִׁ֥יב יָדִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I should soon have subdued their enemies, and turned my hand against their adversaries.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inimici Domini mentiti sunt ei,  et erit tempus eorum in sæcula.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְשַׂנְאֵ֣י יְ֭הֹוָה יְכַחֲשׁוּ־ל֑וֹ וִיהִ֖י עִתָּ֣ם לְעוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: The haters of the Lord should have submitted themselves unto him: but their time should have endured for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cibavit eos ex adipe frumenti,  et de petra melle saturavit eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽ֭יַּאֲכִילֵהוּ מֵחֵ֣לֶב חִטָּ֑ה וּ֝מִצּ֗וּר דְּבַ֣שׁ אַשְׂבִּיעֶֽךָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: He should have fed them also with the finest of the wheat: and with honey out of the rock should I have satisfied thee.
